lrvine, California 92618
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 4'-09-039
Dear Mr. Mendoza:
Re
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the gift limit provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r Our advice is based *t.ty on the facts
presented in your request; the Commission does not act as a finder of fact when
it provides advice. (In re Oglesby (t975) I FPPC Ops. 71.) Also, please nore that our
advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We therefore oif", ,o opinion on the
application, if any, of other laws or your agency's own restrictions on the acceptance of
gitts.
QUESTION
As a non-elected part-time member of a goveming board of a public institution of
higher education, are you prohibited by the Act from accepting gifts over $420 in a
calendar year, and are you required to report such gifts on your Statement of Economic
Interests (Form 700X
CONCLUSION
As a non-elected part-time member of a governing board of a public institution of
higher education you are not prohibited by the Acr from iccepting gifis from a single
source of more than $420 in a calendar year. However, you ur. ,uUi""t to the Act,s
reporting requirements and must report your economic interests inciuding if applicable,
gifts of $50 or more received in a calendar year from a single source, as requiied by your
agency's conflict-of-interest code and disclosure categories applicable to yor.
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Governmenr Code Sections g 1000 through 91014. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections I 8l l0 rhrough I 8997 of Title 2 of the Califirrnia
Code of Regulations. All regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Califbrnia Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. 4-09-039
Page No. 2
You are currently_the managing partner of Mendoza Berger & compan y,L.L.p., a
public accounting firm. You have iecenily been appointed by trr" Gorernoi tole a part
time Trustee for the California State University. A, ,u"h, you are a part time member of
a governing board of a public institution of higher education, and you were not elected to
this position' You are in the process of trying-to complete and file your annual statement
of Economic Interests (Form 700) and woulJlike guidance on how the e.t', grt limits
and reporting requirements apply to you. You would also like guidance on *fr"rh", you
are requiredto report gifts you have received on your statement of Economic Interests if
you are exempt from the Act's gift limit.
rACTS
ANALYSIS
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
-fuIlti, K. fu^A/\-
By: Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, Legal Division
As trustee for the California State. University you are a designated employee of a
state government agency. Normally, designated empioyees are p.ohuit"a uy trre ect
from accepting gifts from a single source i-o *y .a*au. y"ar with a total value of more
than the Act's gift limit if the gift is from a source that is ieportable under the agency,s
conflict-of-interest code. The Act's 2oog-2010 gift limit is currently set at $420 . (See
Regulation 1s940.2) However, subdivision (d) ;f Section 89503 states thar the Act,s gift
Iimit "' ' ' shall not apply to a person in his oi h". 
"upu"iiy 
as a part-time member of the
goveming board of any public institution of higher educaiion unl"r, that position is an
elective office." Therefore, you are not subjeclto the Act's gift limit, u, you have stated
that you are a non-elected part-time membei of the gor"-ing board of a public institution
of higher education. However, even though you are-not subjict to rhe e"i', liit ti-it,
you are required to report your economic interests on your Statement of Ecoiomic
Interests (Form 700). -As a designated employee in yo* ug"ncy's conflict-of-interest
code, you are required to-report gifts you havl receirea toiating $50 or -or" sulle"t to
your agency's conflict-of-interest code and the disclosure categories applicable to your
designated position. (Section 97302(b).)
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
SKB jgl
